input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] The video opens with a close-up of a computer interior featuring a white fan, black braided cooling tubes, and a GIGABYTE motherboard, before cutting to a black screen with white particles and a small white jersey graphic labeled "12". [2-6] The graphic expands into a white silhouette of a figure flexing arms with "SONCK 12" on the jersey back, shifting left as the text "SONCKI" appears to the right, followed by a horizontal line underneath. [6-10] The scene transitions to a display of computer component boxes including a Corsair H100i v2, RM750i power supply, MSI H270 PC MATE motherboard, Intel Core i5 processor, Samsung 850 EVO SSD, Corsair Vengeance RAM, and a Gigabyte G1 Gaming box showing "GEFORCE GTX 1060". [10-12] A person is shown assembling the hardware, with hands working on the MSI motherboard resting on its box, near an Intel Core i5 box, blue-handled pliers, and screws.

recent_thinks: [12-14] The person's hands focus on the center of the MSI motherboard, manipulating the open CPU socket with its retention arm raised. They handle a small component directly over the processor area. The Intel Core i5 box and blue-handled pliers remain on the wooden desk to the right.

[14-16] The individual places the processor into the CPU socket on the MSI motherboard, using both hands to align and secure it. The retention arm is manipulated near the socket area. To the right, the blue Intel Core i5 box and blue-handled pliers sit on the wooden table.

[16-18] The person secures the component by pressing down the black retention arm on the motherboard socket. Their hands then shift, with the left hand holding a small plastic bag near the top left corner of the board. The blue Intel box, white paper, and blue-handled pliers remain stationary on the wooden desk to the right.

[18-20] The scene transitions to the interior of a black computer case with honeycomb mesh panels and a white fan at the top rear. The motherboard is now mounted inside the chassis. A person's hands are adjusting the board, pressing down near the CPU socket and memory slots to secure the installation.

[20-22] Inside the black computer case featuring honeycomb mesh panels and a top-mounted white fan, a person uses both hands to press down on the installed motherboard. The right hand secures the lower section near the expansion slots while the left hand adjusts components near the top rear, ensuring the board is firmly seated within the chassis.

[22-24] Inside the black computer case featuring honeycomb mesh, a person uses a silver screwdriver to secure the motherboard. The tool is applied near the top right edge of the board, fastening it to the case standoffs. The CPU socket remains visible and uncovered as the installation continues within the chassis.

samples.output: <think>Inside the case, a person uses a screwdriver with a green handle to secure the motherboard to the chassis. The tool tightens a screw near the bottom edge of the circuit board. Bundled black cables run along the lower section of the case interior while the board is fixed in place.</think><action>silent</action>

[6-10] The scene transitions to a display of computer component boxes including a Corsair H100i v2, RM750i power supply, MSI H270 PC MATE motherboard, Intel Core i5 processor, Samsung 850 EVO SSD, Corsair Vengeance RAM, and a Gigabyte G1 Gaming box showing "GEFORCE GTX 1060". [10-12] A person is shown assembling the hardware, with hands working on the MSI motherboard resting on its box, near an Intel Core i5 box, blue-handled pliers, and screws.

[14-18] An individual installs a processor into the CPU socket of an MSI motherboard, using both hands to align it before securing the black retention arm. A blue "CORE i5" box, white paper, and blue-handled pliers sit on the wooden table nearby. [18-22] The scene transitions to the interior of a black computer case with honeycomb mesh panels and a white fan. The motherboard is now mounted inside. The person uses both hands to press down on the board near the CPU socket and expansion slots, ensuring it is firmly seated within the chassis. [22-26] Using a screwdriver, first silver then with a green handle, the person secures the motherboard to the case standoffs. They tighten screws near the top right and bottom edges of the board. Bundled black cables run along the lower section of the case interior as the installation is completed.

[26-28] Inside the black computer case, a person uses a green-handled screwdriver to secure the motherboard near the PCIe expansion slots, tightening a screw to fix the board in place while the camera shows the left side of the chassis and the metal expansion slot covers. [28-30] The camera angle adjusts to display the fully installed motherboard within the black chassis, where white metal expansion slot covers line the rear left side and an MSI motherboard box with "H270" text is visible in the background. [30-32] A hand holding the green-handled screwdriver tightens a screw near the CPU socket on the MSI motherboard, revealing the silver heatsink labeled "PRO SERIES" clearly below the socket while the CPU socket remains empty in the center. [32-34] A person uses a knife to slice the tape on a black and yellow box labeled "RM 750i" and "Corsair," which rests on a wooden table beside the open black computer chassis, as hands manipulate the blade and box flap to unseal the power supply packaging.

[38-42] A person positions a black and yellow Corsair box (OCR: "RM 750i") vertically near an open black computer case. A silver multi-tool and green screwdriver rest on the wooden table. The outer packaging is set aside, leaving a plain brown cardboard box lying flat; the person opens the top flap displaying the "CORSAIR" logo in black ink.
[42-46] The brown box opens to reveal white instruction manuals and black cables nestled inside. The individual removes a white manual listing "RM 1000i", "RM 850i", "RM 750i", and "RM 650i". Finally, the black power supply unit is lifted out of the packaging, leaving cables inside the box next to the chassis.

recent_thinks: [12-14] The person's hands focus on the center of the MSI motherboard, manipulating the open CPU socket with its retention arm raised. They handle a small component directly over the processor area. The Intel Core i5 box and blue-handled pliers remain on the wooden desk to the right.

[34-36] A person uses a silver knife to cut the tape on a black and yellow box labeled 'RM 750i' and 'CORSAIR'. The box rests on a wooden table beside the open black computer chassis. Hands manipulate the packaging, pulling at the plastic wrap to unseal the power supply unit.

[36-38] The individual rotates the black and yellow Corsair power supply box, displaying the rear panel with technical specifications and diagrams. The silver knife rests on the wooden table surface. Both hands grip the packaging next to the open black computer case structure.

[46-48] After setting aside a white manual, the individual lifts the black power supply unit, encased in a protective bag, out of the brown cardboard box. Bundled cables remain visible inside the packaging on the wooden table, situated next to the open black computer chassis.

[48-50] The individual holds a black power supply unit wrapped in clear plastic in their right hand and a black fabric bag in their left hand. The open brown cardboard box rests on the wooden table, positioned next to the black computer chassis structure on the right side of the frame.

The protective plastic wrapping is gone, and a black fabric bag with a white logo lies on the table near the open computer case frame.</think><action>silent</action>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] The video opens with a close-up of a computer interior featuring a white fan, black braided cooling tubes, and a GIGABYTE motherboard, before cutting to a black screen with white particles and a small white jersey graphic labeled "12". [2-6] The graphic expands into a white silhouette of a figure flexing arms with "SONCK 12" on the jersey back, shifting left as the text "SONCKI" appears to the right, followed by a horizontal line underneath. [6-10] The scene transitions to a display of computer component boxes including a Corsair H100i v2, RM750i power supply, MSI H270 PC MATE motherboard, Intel Core i5 processor, Samsung 850 EVO SSD, Corsair Vengeance RAM, and a Gigabyte G1 Gaming box showing "GEFORCE GTX 1060". [10-12] A person is shown assembling the hardware, with hands working on the MSI motherboard resting on its box, near an Intel Core i5 box, blue-handled pliers, and screws.

[14-18] An individual installs a processor into the CPU socket of an MSI motherboard, using both hands to align it before securing the black retention arm. A blue "CORE i5" box, white paper, and blue-handled pliers sit on the wooden table nearby. [18-22] The scene transitions to the interior of a black computer case with honeycomb mesh panels and a white fan. The motherboard is now mounted inside. The person uses both hands to press down on the board near the CPU socket and expansion slots, ensuring it is firmly seated within the chassis. [22-26] Using a screwdriver, first silver then with a green handle, the person secures the motherboard to the case standoffs. They tighten screws near the top right and bottom edges of the board. Bundled black cables run along the lower section of the case interior as the installation is completed.

[26-28] Inside the black computer case, a person uses a green-handled screwdriver to secure the motherboard near the PCIe expansion slots, tightening a screw to fix the board in place while the camera shows the left side of the chassis and the metal expansion slot covers. [28-30] The camera angle adjusts to display the fully installed motherboard within the black chassis, where white metal expansion slot covers line the rear left side and an MSI motherboard box with "H270" text is visible in the background. [30-32] A hand holding the green-handled screwdriver tightens a screw near the CPU socket on the MSI motherboard, revealing the silver heatsink labeled "PRO SERIES" clearly below the socket while the CPU socket remains empty in the center. [32-34] A person uses a knife to slice the tape on a black and yellow box labeled "RM 750i" and "Corsair," which rests on a wooden table beside the open black computer chassis, as hands manipulate the blade and box flap to unseal the power supply packaging.

[38-42] A person positions a black and yellow Corsair box (OCR: "RM 750i") vertically near an open black computer case. A silver multi-tool and green screwdriver rest on the wooden table. The outer packaging is set aside, leaving a plain brown cardboard box lying flat; the person opens the top flap displaying the "CORSAIR" logo in black ink.
[42-46] The brown box opens to reveal white instruction manuals and black cables nestled inside. The individual removes a white manual listing "RM 1000i", "RM 850i", "RM 750i", and "RM 650i". Finally, the black power supply unit is lifted out of the packaging, leaving cables inside the box next to the chassis.

[46-50] After setting aside a manual, an individual lifts a black power supply unit, wrapped in protective plastic, from a brown cardboard box on a wooden table next to an open black computer chassis. Bundled cables are visible inside the box. [50-52] The person holds the unwrapped black unit with both hands, revealing the white "CORSAIR" logo and "RM750i" model text on the side panel. A black fabric bag with a white logo rests on the table near the case frame. [52-56] The unit is now installed in the rear bottom of the black chassis. The exterior back panel displays the power socket, switch, and honeycomb grill labeled "RM750i." A hand touches the rear of the case near the installed unit, confirming its secure placement within the structure.

recent_thinks: [12-14] The person's hands focus on the center of the MSI motherboard, manipulating the open CPU socket with its retention arm raised. They handle a small component directly over the processor area. The Intel Core i5 box and blue-handled pliers remain on the wooden desk to the right.

[34-36] A person uses a silver knife to cut the tape on a black and yellow box labeled 'RM 750i' and 'CORSAIR'. The box rests on a wooden table beside the open black computer chassis. Hands manipulate the packaging, pulling at the plastic wrap to unseal the power supply unit.

[36-38] The individual rotates the black and yellow Corsair power supply box, displaying the rear panel with technical specifications and diagrams. The silver knife rests on the wooden table surface. Both hands grip the packaging next to the open black computer case structure.

[56-58] A hand utilizes a green-handled screwdriver to secure the black power supply unit to the rear of the computer chassis by tightening a screw at the bottom right corner. The unit displays a honeycomb grill pattern and 'RM750i' text near the power switch.

[58-60] A hand utilizes a green-handled screwdriver to secure a screw into the top left mounting hole of the black power supply unit. The rear panel features a honeycomb grill, power switch, and socket, with the model text "RM750i" visible. Above the unit, white expansion slot covers are attached to the black chassis frame.

[60-62] A hand utilizes a green-handled screwdriver to secure the top left screw of the black power supply unit at the rear of the chassis. The unit features a honeycomb grill and "RM750i" text, sitting below white expansion slot covers as the installation is finalized.

queries.question: Has the motherboard brand changed from the beginning of the video to the later assembly stages?

Fingers apply pressure to ensure the memory modules are fully seated next to the CPU socket area on the black circuit board. Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] The video opens with a close-up of a computer interior featuring a white fan, black braided cooling tubes, and a GIGABYTE motherboard, before cutting to a black screen with white particles and a small white jersey graphic labeled "12". [2-6] The graphic expands into a white silhouette of a figure flexing arms with "SONCK 12" on the jersey back, shifting left as the text "SONCKI" appears to the right, followed by a horizontal line underneath. [6-10] The scene transitions to a display of computer component boxes including a Corsair H100i v2, RM750i power supply, MSI H270 PC MATE motherboard, Intel Core i5 processor, Samsung 850 EVO SSD, Corsair Vengeance RAM, and a Gigabyte G1 Gaming box showing "GEFORCE GTX 1060". [10-12] A person is shown assembling the hardware, with hands working on the MSI motherboard resting on its box, near an Intel Core i5 box, blue-handled pliers, and screws. [14-18] An individual installs a processor into the CPU socket of an MSI motherboard, using both hands to align it before securing the black retention arm. A blue "CORE i5" box, white paper, and blue-handled pliers sit on the wooden table nearby. [18-22] The scene transitions to the interior of

[36-38] An individual rotates a black and yellow Corsair power supply box showing the rear panel, with a silver knife on the table and hands gripping packaging near the open black computer case. [56-58] A hand uses a green-handled screwdriver to secure the black power supply unit to the rear chassis by tightening a bottom right screw; the unit features a honeycomb grill and 'RM750i' text. [66-70] Inside the chassis, hands organize black cables above the installed Corsair RM750i unit and vertically mounted MSI motherboard, routing wiring through an opening in the metal chassis wall near the mounting plate.

Inside the black chassis, hands work near the top edge of the black msi H270 PC MATE motherboard, manipulating a black cable connector above the silver CPU socket. A white fan is mounted on the left side panel, and bundled black wires run vertically along the right interior wall next to the mounting plate which features white star-shaped markings. The person appears to be routing or connecting a cable near the upper edge of the board. A close-up view shows the hand connecting the black cable to the top left header, adjacent to the silver CPU socket holding a gold processor. White circuit traces decorate the board surface as the hand manipulates the connector near the processor area.

[80-88] The camera zooms in on a black motherboard where a gold processor is installed in a silver socket. A hand reaches from the top left, manipulating a component or cable near the CPU area and upper edge. To the left, large white fan blades are mounted on the case panel. The motherboard displays white circuit traces and "msi" text near the bottom edge. As the view tightens, vertical text reading "DDR4 BOOST" becomes clearly visible on the right side of the board.

[88-96] The camera zooms in on a black motherboard, displaying vertical white text "DDR4 BOOST" on the right and "msi" branding at the bottom center. A gold processor sits in a silver socket. Large white fan blades occupy the left foreground. A hand manipulates a component near the top edge, just above the processor. The camera zooms closer, centering on the gold processor in the silver socket. The hand reaches from the left to adjust a component near the top edge of the CPU area. Throughout the sequence, the hand reaches into the black case, manipulating components and adjusting wiring near the top edge of the motherboard above the gold processor. The white fan blades remain visible on the left panel. The vertical "DDR4 BOOST" text and "msi" logo are consistently visible as the person works near the silver CPU socket.

[34-36] A person uses a silver knife to cut the tape on a black and yellow box labeled 'RM 750i' and 'CORSAIR'. The box rests on a wooden table beside the open black computer chassis. Hands manipulate the packaging, pulling at the plastic wrap to unseal the power supply unit.

[70-72] Inside the black chassis, the view focuses on the motherboard with its exposed silver CPU socket. A hand reaches toward the upper section of the board, near the processor area. [96-98] A hand reaches into the black chassis, hovering over the top section of the black motherboard where a gold processor sits in a silver socket. White fan blades are mounted on the left panel, and vertical "DDR4 BOOST" text is visible on the right side. The "msi" logo is centered at the bottom as the hand manipulates components near the upper edge of the board.

[98-100] Inside the black case, a hand works near the top of the black motherboard, adjacent to the gold processor in the silver socket. Large white fan blades occupy the left foreground. Vertical white text reading "DDR4 BOOST" appears on the right side of the board, while the "msi" logo sits at the bottom center. The hand adjusts a component near the CPU area.
